metadata.summary: PMBoK 8 organises project management into seven domains: Governance, Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, Risk. Each maps to artefacts and measurable outcomes. Fixed canonical list.

content.title: Seven Performance Domains (PMBoK 8)
content.summary: PMBoK 8 reorganises project management into seven performance domains: Governance, Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, Risk. Quality, Communications, and Procurement are integrated throughout rather than standalone domains. Each domain maps to observable artefacts and measurable outcomes. The canonical domain list is fixed — agents must never invent an 8th domain or reintroduce "Integration Management" or "Quality" as standalone items.

why.p: Domains are the smallest mental model that covers the full scope of a project. Each project artefact belongs under exactly one domain, making gap analysis mechanical: walk the seven, check artefact presence, flag missing ones as risks. PMBoK 8 replaces PMBoK 7's eight domains (Stakeholder, Team, Development Approach, Planning, Project Work, Delivery, Measurement, Uncertainty) and PMBoK 6's ten knowledge areas, so teams migrating between editions need a translation layer.

list.item: Structuring a project charter or status pack around a known taxonomy (charter → closure)
Auditing an existing project plan: walk each domain and surface missing artefacts
Building a weekly project status dashboard (one panel per domain, RAG per outcome)
Tailoring methodology selection — domains are stable; processes inside them vary by approach
Onboarding a PM agent: seven domains is the smallest anchor that still covers everything
Pure-Scrum teams with Product Owner / Scrum Master where Scrum events already cover domains implicitly — domain mapping is busywork
Solopreneur projects under 1 week with a single deliverable — governance and finance collapse to "did I ship, did I get paid"
Shops still on PMBoK 6 (process groups + knowledge areas) in mid-project — switching narrative mid-project confuses sponsors
Domain-driven design conversations — the word "domain" overloads the term

rule.statement: Always use exactly these seven domain names — never add an 8th, never reintroduce "Integration Management", "Quality", or "Communications" as standalone domains.
rule.rationale: PMBoK 8 explicitly integrates Quality, Communications, and Procurement. LLMs trained on PMBoK 6 data will hallucinate these as standalone domains; the canonical seven must be pinned in system prompts.

list.item: Governance — oversight, decision frameworks, value realisation; replaces Integration Management; includes sustainability as a core metric
Scope — define and control what is included and excluded; enhanced definition and traceability
Schedule — plan and manage timelines, milestones, and dependencies
Finance — budgets, costs, financial performance; replaces Cost Management; pairs with EVM
Stakeholders — identify, analyse, and engage all stakeholders; now includes Communications
Resources — acquire and manage team members and physical resources; enhanced focus on team development
Risk — identify, analyse, and respond to uncertainties including threats and opportunities

section.name: Key Artefacts per Domain
section.p: Minimal artefact set that satisfies each domain. A missing artefact is a risk in the Risk domain.

list.item: Governance: charter.md, decision-log.md
Scope: scope.md, wbs.md, requirements-traceability.md
Schedule: schedule.md, milestones.md
Finance: budget.md, cost-baseline.md
Stakeholders: stakeholder-register.md, comms-plan.md, engagement-plan.md
Resources: raci.md, team-charter.md
Risk: risk-register.md
Principles: 12 (PMBoK 7) → 6 streamlined (PMBoK 8): Stewardship and Ethics, Collaboration, Value Optimisation, Systems and Complexity, Adaptability, Sustainability

Domains: 8 (PMBoK 7) → 7 (PMBoK 8): Team + Project Work collapsed into Resources + Governance; Planning + Delivery merged into Scope/Schedule/Finance; Measurement absorbed into respective domains; Uncertainty → Risk
Sustainability: mentioned in PMBoK 7, core principle in PMBoK 8 — needs a measurable proxy (e.g. CO2 per release, accessibility pass rate)
AI Coverage: none in PMBoK 7, dedicated appendix in PMBoK 8

rule.statement: Walk the seven domains in fixed order (Governance, Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, Risk) and for each emit: artefact_path (or MISSING), owner (or UNASSIGNED), last_updated (ISO date or never), status (green/amber/red), next_action (one imperative sentence).
rule.rationale: Fixed domain order makes diffs across weekly runs stable — status changes are visible as line-level changes in version control without noise from reordering.

rule.statement: Run the domain audit before every steering committee meeting. The output is the status pack, not a separate document that the PM then summarises manually.
rule.rationale: Separate manual summarisation introduces optimism bias and lag. An automated audit from source artefacts is objective and current.

rule.statement: Map each missing domain artefact directly to a risk entry in the Risk domain register. A missing artefact is not a process gap — it is a project risk with a probability and impact.
rule.rationale: Risk registers that do not include process gaps undercount project risk systematically. Governance gaps are often higher-impact than delivery risks.

antipattern.description: LLM output contains "Integration Management" or "Quality Management" as a domain name.
antipattern.reason: These are PMBoK 6 terms; in PMBoK 8 they are integrated. Any output containing these names is anchored to the wrong edition and must be rejected.

antipattern.description: Agent output lists eight domains (adding Quality or Communications as a standalone).
antipattern.reason: Validate output length == 7 domains. An 8th domain signals PMBoK 6 training bias; reject and re-prompt with the canonical seven in the system prompt.

antipattern.description: Domain status colours drift to "green" over time without human sampling.
antipattern.reason: Agents self-validate by confirming artefact existence, not artefact quality. Calibrate by sampling 1 in 5 domains for human review each cycle.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Use the seven domains as a top-level rubric the agent walks linearly: for each domain, the agent asks "what artefact exists, who owns it, what is the current state?". Output is a markdown matrix (domain × artefact × owner × status × next-action). Re-run weekly; diff drives the status report. Never let the agent invent domain names — pin them to the canonical seven (Governance, Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, Risk).

Recommended subagents: pm-domain-auditor (define inline) walks the seven domains against a project workspace and produces a gap matrix. faion-sdd-executor drives implementation of domain gaps through quality gates. brainstorm generates initial artefacts for a new domain. nero-improve feeds the gap matrix into periodic remediation loops.

section.name: Limitations
section.p: Domains are descriptive, not generative: knowing "Risk" is a domain does not produce a risk register; it points you at the methodology. "Governance replaces Integration Management" is not a 1:1 swap; portfolio-level governance was previously implicit and now leaks into PM scope, surprising old PMP holders. "Quality integrated throughout" sounds elegant but hides quality work; agents skip explicit quality plans because the domain is invisible.

Stakeholder + Communications fusion drops dedicated comms artefacts (comms plan, RACI for messages); teams forget to recreate them. Procurement moved to appendix → agents under-emphasize vendor risk on contractor-heavy projects. Sustainability is named but unmeasured in PMBoK 8 itself; teams need external frameworks (GHG Protocol, B-Corp) to operationalize.

section.name: CLI Tools and Services
section.p: Key tools for domain auditing and management:

list.item: gh — Pull issues / PRs as scope + schedule signal
jira-cli — Read epics / stories per domain
linear CLI / API — Stakeholder + scope sync
monday-cli (community) — Resources + risk boards
gitleaks / trufflehog — Governance / sec hygiene per domain audit
notion-sdk (Python/JS) — Pull domain artefacts from a project workspace
mermaid-cli — Render domain interaction diagrams

section.p: SaaS platforms: Jira Premium / Advanced Roadmaps (Scope + Schedule + Resources), ClickUp (Stakeholders + Resources), Smartsheet (Governance forums + Finance dashboards), Monday.com (Risk + Resources boards), Notion / Confluence (Domain charter + status doc), Power BI / Looker / Metabase (Finance + Measurement dashboards), MS Project / Project for the Web (Schedule + Resources baseline), Asana Portfolios (Governance roll-up).

list.item: Treat domains as the project's table of contents; every artefact lives under exactly one domain
Pair each domain with a measurable outcome (e.g. Finance → "CPI ≥ 0.95 at every gate"), not just an activity
Run the seven-domain audit before every steering committee — drives the status pack automatically
When a process feels homeless, default to Governance; do not invent a new domain
Map domain gaps to risks: a missing artefact = a risk in the Risk domain
For PMBoK 7 → 8 transitions, keep a translation table next to the audit so old PMs do not wave the integration-management flag
Track domain coverage as a project KPI (e.g. "7/7 green" by gate 2)
LLMs blur Stakeholders + Communications back into separate domains because PMBoK 6 is heavily represented in training data; pin the canonical seven in the system prompt
Agents will silently rename Governance to "Integration Management"; reject any output containing the old name
"Quality" is integrated, not a domain — agents keep adding an 8th domain. Validate output length == 7
Sustainability creeps into Stakeholders or Risk inconsistently; either keep it under Governance or as an explicit sub-criterion in every domain — pick one and lock it
Agents underweight Procurement on vendor-heavy projects because PMBoK 8 demoted it; force a vendor-risk question into the audit
Domain status colors drift over time (every artefact "green") — calibrate by sampling 1 in 5 domains for human review
Output ordering matters: list domains in fixed sequence so diffs across runs are stable

template.purpose: Python: walk seven domains against a project root, emit JSON gap matrix
